source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A thin spherical shell of radius $R$ has a fixed charge $+q$ distributed uniformly over its surface. A small circular section (radius $r \ll R$) of charge is removed from the surface. The cut section is replaced and the sphere is set in motion rotating with constant angular velocity $\omega=\omega_{0}$ about the $z$-axis. Now the sphere's angular velocity increases linearly with time: \(\omega=\omega_{0}+kt\). Calculate the line integral of the electric field around the circular path $P$ (located at the center of the sphere with normal along the $+z$ axis and radius $r_{P} \ll R$).
extraction_notes: force_requires_image inferred from raw_record.vision_relevance="essential" and figure references in reasoning/caption
image entries are PIL object representations, not concrete file paths or URLs

image_dependency: 上游字段 `requires_image=true` 且 `text_dominant=false`，说明样本被登记为偏图像依赖。
题干涉及球壳、挖去小圆面、路径 P 的空间位置与法向，存在几何关系展示需求。
但题干文字已给出关键几何与物理设定（半径、轴向、路径位置），因此不判到接近 1 的强图像刚需。
multi_step: 题目不是直接读数，需结合缺失电荷区域、转动带来的磁效应、以及 `omega=omega_0+kt` 的时间变化来推到环路电场积分。
需要至少两步以上物理推理：先确定等效电流/磁场变化，再利用感应电场环量关系。
答案为解析表达式而非选择题，支持较长推导链；题干文本长度也较长（`question_char_length=588`）。
verifiability: 标准答案是明确的短公式（`answer_type=short_text`），目标输出形式清晰。
该类结果可做符号级或量纲级核验，较适合后续规则或求解器比对。
资产登记通过（`registry_passed=true`），且题干与答案都存在，降低了样本不完整导致的不可核验风险。

物理推导细节未在登记信息中展开，过程评分仍可能受解法差异影响

初步评分整体较高，尤其是可核验性强（答案为明确短公式），适合进入候选池。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A thin spherical shell of radius $R$ has a fixed charge $+q$ distributed uniformly over its surface. A small circular section (radius $r \ll R$) of charge is removed from the surface. The cut section is replaced and the sphere is set in motion rotating with constant angular velocity $\omega=\omega_{0}$ about the $z$-axis. Now the sphere's angular velocity increases linearly with time: \(\omega=\omega_{0}+kt\). Calculate the line integral of the electric field around the circular path $P$ (located at the center of the sphere with normal along the $+z$ axis and radius $r_{P} \ll R$).
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: force_requires_image inferred from raw_record.vision_relevance="essential" and figure references in reasoning/caption
image entries are PIL object representations, not concrete file paths or URLs
metadata.reasoning_chain: Consider a ring of width $R\,d\theta$ as shown in Fig. 2.25. The surface current density on the ring is $\frac{\theta}{4 \pi R^{2}} \cdot \omega R \sin\theta$. The contributions of a pair of symmetrical points on the ring to the magnetic field at the center of the sphere will sum up to a resultant in the $z$-direction. Thus the total contribution of the ring to the magnetic field is given by the Biot-Savart law as $$dB_{z}=\frac{\mu_{0}}{4 \pi}\int_{\varphi=0}^{2\pi}\frac{q}{4\pi R^{2}}\omega R \sin\theta\cdot\frac{R}{R^{3}}\sin\theta R\,d\theta\cdot R\sin\theta\,d\varphi=\frac{\mu_{0}q\omega}{8\pi R}\sin^{3}\theta\,d\theta,$$ Hence $$B_{z}=\frac{\mu_{0}q\omega}{8\pi R}\int_{0}^{\pi}\sin^{3}\theta\,d\theta=\frac{\mu_{0}q\omega}{6\pi R}.$$ As $r_{P} \ll R$, the magnetic induction can be taken to be uniform in the circular loop $P$. The magnetic flux crossing $P$ is then $$\phi=\pi r_{P}^{2}B=\frac{\mu_{0}r_{P}^{2}\omega q}{6R}=\frac{\mu_{0}q r_{P}^{2}(\omega_{0}+kt)}{6R}.$$ Hence $$\oint_{P}\mathbf{E}\cdot d\mathbf{l}=-\frac{d\phi}{dt}=\frac{\mu_{0}kr_{P}^{2}q}{6R}.$$

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain: Consider a ring of width $R\,d\theta$ as shown in Fig. 2.25. The surface current density on the ring is $\frac{\theta}{4 \pi R^{2}} \cdot \omega R \sin\theta$. The contributions of a pair of symmetrical points on the ring to the magnetic field at the center of the sphere will sum up to a resultant in the $z$-direction. Thus the total contribution of the ring to the magnetic field is given by the Biot-Savart law as $$dB_{z}=\frac{\mu_{0}}{4 \pi}\int_{\varphi=0}^{2\pi}\frac{q}{4\pi R^{2}}\omega R \sin\theta\cdot\frac{R}{R^{3}}\sin\theta R\,d\theta\cdot R\sin\theta\,d\varphi=\frac{\mu_{0}q\omega}{8\pi R}\sin^{3}\theta\,d\theta,$$ Hence $$B_{z}=\frac{\mu_{0}q\omega}{8\pi R}\int_{0}^{\pi}\sin^{3}\theta\,d\theta=\frac{\mu_{0}q\omega}{6\pi R}.$$ As $r_{P} \ll R$, the magnetic induction can be taken to be uniform in the circular loop $P$. The magnetic flux crossing $P$ is then $$\phi=\pi r_{P}^{2}B=\frac{\mu_{0}r_{P}^{2}\omega q}{6R}=\frac{\mu_{0}q r_{P}^{2}(\omega_{0}+kt)}{6R}.$$ Hence $$\oint_{P}\mathbf{E}\cdot d\mathbf{l}=-\frac{d\phi}{dt}=\frac{\mu_{0}kr_{P}^{2}q}{6R}.$$
